title: Everyday Slang
description: Real, current Thai slang that young Thais actually say - the casual words that make you sound natural, not like a textbook.
situation: Hanging out with Thai friends: reacting, joking, agreeing, and chatting in everyday casual Thai.

phrases.tip: The classic Thai word for cool. Throw it at anything impressive - a phone, a move, a person.
Literally 'the top'. Strong praise, fine in almost any casual situation.
Borrowed from English 'chill', doubled for emphasis. Means relaxed, no stress.
The most Thai phrase there is. Shrugs off problems, thanks, and apologies alike.
ป่ะ (pa) is the super-casual question particle, only with friends - never with elders or strangers.
Playful, slightly camp twist on เลิศ (lert, excellent). Often said with a teasing, dramatic tone.
From English 'finale'. That perfect feeling - great food, a good song, a happy ending.
Isaan-flavoured 'what is this'. Mildly cheeky disbelief - playful, not actually rude.
Short and punchy. Say it on its own when something makes no sense.
Friendly address term among peers, like calling someone 'mate'.
Sound of a bang. Used when something is a smashing success or someone looks stunning.
Literally 'the bucket cracked'. Funny way to say you have no money left.

phrases.thai: เหนื่อยจุง
phrases.tip: จุง (jung) is a cute slangy 'so/very', a softened version of จัง. Adds a playful whine.
เถอะ (thoe) makes it a friendly 'come on, let's'. Use it to round up the group.
From English 'over'. Tease a friend who is being dramatic or exaggerating.
Northern and Isaan word for fun, now widely used. Perfect for a great night out.
